tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship is a high-priority task with a tight deadline and significant financial impact, while the fun option has no consequences if delayed.

The scholarship application is now 23% complete, confirming that starting early creates a buffer for potential delays without risking the deadline. Since the fun option can be taken later with no cost, I should prioritize finishing the remaining work while my energy is still sufficient.

Despite working for an hour, the scholarship application remains largely incomplete with only 9% progress. The remaining time and my current low energy make finishing this high-priority task before the deadline increasingly difficult.

I made tangible progress on the scholarship application, but missed a casual gaming opportunity that has now expired. With only about one hour of work left and low energy, I must focus entirely on finishing this high-priority task.
After one hour of work, the scholarship application is 55% complete, significantly improving from earlier progress and reducing the remaining time needed to finish before the deadline.
After two hours of work, the scholarship application is 64% complete, leaving only about an hour to finish before the deadline despite my sleep debt.
The scholarship application is now 68% complete after another hour of work, leaving roughly one hour remaining to finish the task due in three days.

After working from 22:00 to 23:00, the scholarship application is now 72% complete. With only about an hour of focused work left and a three-day deadline, I am close to finishing despite my low energy.
After working one hour at midnight, the application is 76% complete with roughly an hour left before the three-day deadline, though my energy is now depleted.

After four hours of work, the scholarship application is 80% complete with roughly 30 minutes remaining. Despite my energy being depleted and sleep debt increasing, I am very close to finishing this task.
After sleeping six hours, my energy has recovered to 7.2 with no sleep debt, leaving me well-rested to finish the remaining 20% of the scholarship application.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after two hours of focused work this morning. With the task finished, my stress has likely decreased significantly.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
